scenario: CRUD API design with basic data model

constraints: No breaking changes to existing auth
input.goal: Allow users to read and update their profiles

expected_behavior: skill should produce design with: (1) User schema with id/email/name/createdAt, (2) GET /api/users/{id}, PUT /api/users/{id}, (3) Sequence diagram showing happy path; (4) Error cases (404, 400)
notes: Tests standard CRUD design with clear contract boundaries

scenario: Microservice integration requiring new endpoint + schema migration

input.feature: Add payment processing to order flow
constraints: Must not change existing Order schema directly
input.goal: Integrate payment service with order checkout

expected_behavior: skill should design: (1) new Payment schema (id/orderId/amount/status), (2) POST /api/orders/{id}/payment endpoint, (3) sequence diagram showing payment → order state transition, (4) identify delta changes (OrderService adds paymentId FK, PaymentService new)
notes: Tests inter-service communication and breaking change awareness

constraints: RULES.md forbids direct DB access from UI layer

expected_behavior: skill should REJECT design and return status BLOCKED with message: 'Proposed direct DB access from PaymentUI violates RULES.md § database-access-boundary; must route through service layer'
notes: Tests drift detection: ensures design respects architectural ADRs
